findings.before: Five exact duplicate groups and six excess hero assignments made unrelated modules visually interchangeable.
findings.after: Fifteen unique registered hero assets plus one approved leadership portrait composition; zero duplicated principal heroes.

findings.before: Eleven non-product modules used Product Portfolio photography as a generic corporate fallback.
findings.after: Product photography is limited to product-led placements; legacy CSS fallbacks were removed and are regression-tested.

findings.before: Generic illustration-led flow with weak route differentiation.
findings.after: Three distinct photographic pathways, explicit status labels, regulatory disclosures and one governed convergence.

findings.before: One repeated image, flat cards, empty space and weak hierarchy.
findings.after: Four image-led qualification pathways with unique numbering, evidence boundaries and representative-media disclosure.

findings.before: Decorative or generic visual treatments did not communicate controlled process or system maturity.
findings.after: A staged regulatory control track and photographic technology architecture show sequence, status and accountability.

findings.before: Editorial SVGs and a Product Portfolio hero weakened article differentiation.
findings.after: Six article-specific responsive image sets and a unique evidence-led Insights hero are connected to social and image metadata.
